analyst_observations: with_skill sweeps 3/3 with 100% pass rate vs 54.8% average for old_skill
Eval 2 (standard endpoint) is the decisive win: 100% vs 14.3% — old skill runs 11 stages for a clear-scope 4-file task, new skill routes to 3 stages
Eval 1 (typo) both handle well — with_skill adds signal assessment table, old_skill uses simpler Micro fast path
Eval 3 (large feature) both route correctly to full pipeline — with_skill adds explicit signal assessment, old_skill uses tier matching
The STANDARD path is the entire value proposition: it fills the gap between 'trivial typo' and 'build a whole system' that the old skill didn't have
Old skill forces a binary choice: MICRO (skip everything) or FULL (run everything). The STANDARD path is the missing middle ground
Signal assessment table provides audit trail for WHY a path was chosen — old skill just says 'this is Medium'
